segments.text: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked,
Who then is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Jesus invited a little child to st and among them.
Truly I tell you,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said,
unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore, whoever humbles himself like this little child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And whoever welcomes a little child like this in My name welcomes Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But if anyone causes one of these little ones who believe in Me to stumble, it would be better for him to have a large millstone hung around his neck and to be drowned in the depths of the sea.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to the world for the causes of sin. These stumbling blocks must come, but woe to the man through whom they come!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If your hand or your foot causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. It is better for you to enter life crippled or lame than to have two hands and two feet and be thrown into the eternal fire.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And if your eye causes you to sin, gouge it out and throw it away. It is better for you to enter life with one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fire of hell.
segments.speaker: Jesus

text: The Parable of the Lost Sheep

segments.text: See that you do not look down on any of these little ones. For I tell you that their angels in heaven always see the face of My Father in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What do you think? If a man has a hundred sheep and one of them goes astray, will he not leave the ninety-nine on the hills and go out to search for the one that is lost?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And if he finds it, truly I tell you, he rejoices more over that one sheep than over the ninety-nine that did not go astray.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In the same way, your Father in heaven is not willing that any of these little ones should perish.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If your brother sins against you, go and confront him privately. If he listens to you, you have won your brother over.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But if he will not listen, take one or two others along, so that ‘every matter may be established by the testimony of two or three witnesses.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If he refuses to listen to them, tell it to the church. And if he refuses to listen even to the church, regard him as you would a pagan or a tax collector.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Truly I tell you, whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Again, I tell you truly that if two of you on the earth agree about anything you ask for, it will be done for you by My Father in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For where two or three gather together in My name, there am I with them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Peter came to Jesus and asked,
Lord, how many times shall I forgive my brother who sins against me? Up to seven times?
segments.speaker: Peter came to Jesus and

segments.text: Jesus answered,
I tell you, not just seven times, but seventy-seven times!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Because of this, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As he began the settlements, a debtor was brought to him owing ten thousand talents.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Since the man was unable to pay, the master ordered that he be sold to pay his debt, along with his wife and children and everything he owned.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the servant fell on his knees before him. ‘Have patience with me,’ he begged, ‘and I will pay back everything.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: His master had compassion on him, forgave his debt, and released him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But when that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him a hundred denarii. He grabbed him and began to choke him, saying, ‘Pay back what you owe me!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So his fellow servant fell down and begged him, ‘Have patience with me, and I will pay you back.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But he refused. Instead, he went and had the man thrown into prison until he could pay his debt.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When his fellow servants saw what had happened, they were greatly distressed, and they went and recounted all of this to their master.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the master summoned him and declared, ‘You wicked servant! I forgave all your debt because you begged me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Shouldn’t you have had mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had on you?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In anger his master turned him over to the jailers to be tortured, until he should repay all that he owed.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: That is how My heavenly Father will treat each of you unless you forgive your brother from your heart.
segments.speaker: Jesus
